page: Microsoft Azure DC32ds_v3 is a member of the DDCSv3 server family, utilizing the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is equipped with an Intel Xeon 8370C processor featuring 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs running at a base clock speed of 2.8 GHz. The CPU architecture is x86_64, supported by a cache hierarchy consisting of 48 KB L1d cache, 1 MB L2 cache, and 48 MB L3 cache. The system includes 256.0 GB of RAM, providing a memory density of 8.0 GB per core. It features 2458 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, though it does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong performance in multi-core and specialized computing tasks. The server achieved a Geekbench multi-core score of 17264.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 49816.66. Cryptographic and extended instruction capabilities are notable, with a Passmark CPU encryption test score of 36927.97 and an extended instructions test score of 53627.12, both categorized as strong. In memory-intensive operations, the system recorded a read-only small-block memory bandwidth of 3375.16 GB/sec and a large-block write bandwidth of 69.27 GB/sec. Database operations performed strongly, yielding 25047.25 on the Passmark database test, while static web serving throughput reached 48.38 GB/sec.

Given its 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and substantial 2458 GB local storage, the DC32ds_v3 is highly suited for memory-intensive workloads, database management, and static web serving. Its cryptographic hardware acceleration and strong multi-core performance make it efficient for secure data processing and image processing. However, for large-scale LLM text generation, the server exhibits weaker performance, as evidenced by a 2.64 tokens/sec rate on a 70B model. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the combination of dedicated Intel Xeon cores, high memory density, and integrated local storage provides a balanced resource profile for enterprise databases and memory-optimized applications without requiring separate storage attachments.
description: Microsoft Azure DC32ds_v3 is an x86_64 server from the DDCSv3 family, powered by a 32-core Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 256.0 GB of RAM (8.0 GB per core) and 2458 GB of local storage, with Premium Storage capabilities and no integrated GPUs. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, it delivers strong multi-core performance, as demonstrated by a Geekbench multi-core score of 17264.0 and a Passmark database score of 25047.25. The server also exhibits strong static web serving throughput of 48.38 GB/sec and robust cryptographic capabilities. It is highly suited for database operations, memory-intensive applications, and secure data processing, though it shows limitations in large-scale LLM text generation.
og_description: Microsoft Azure DC32ds_v3 is a memory-optimized server featuring 32 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 256 GB of RAM, and 2458 GB of local storage, delivering strong performance for database and web serving workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure DC32ds_v3 is a 32-vCPU Intel Xeon server with 256 GB RAM and 2458 GB local storage, optimized for database and memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 server featuring thirty-two dedicated Intel Xeon cores, high-density RAM, and substantial local storage for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C cores running at 2.8 GHz
256 GB of RAM providing 8.0 GB per core memory density
2458 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong performance in database operations and static web serving
